perturbation.original_text: Before submitting a Measured Enterprise’s Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, B EE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email and will disclose the information within 7 days after Measured Enterprise has been notified BEE Online
perturbation.changed_text: Before submitting a Client's Confidential Information when requested by DTI, SANAS or required by Law, B EE Online shall notify the Measured Enterprise within 2 days of receiving the written request by email and will disclose the information within 7 days after Measured Enterprise has been notified BEE Online
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Measured Enterprise's' with 'Client's' implies that BEE Online only protects the confidential information of its direct clients and not necessarily the 'Measured Enterprise'. This contradicts the initial definition of parties in Section 1, creating an in-text contradiction since the 'client' is now a separate entity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Between BEE ONLINE (PTY) LTD (“BEE Online ”) Registration number: 2004/004257/07 And (“the company or individual” ) Registration/Identity Number: (“the parties”) Doc No: BO 115 Last Modified: 11 July 2014 Approved By: Executive Committee Revision No.: 07 BO115 -07 - Non-Disclosure Agreement 11 July 2014 Page 2 of 5 1. INTRODUCTION This document recognizes that BEE Online has entered into a contract with the named individual or organization in the normal course of our business.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Measured Enterprise's' to 'Client's' introduces a significant contradiction. The original text implies that BEE Online protects the confidential information of the Measured Enterprise. The changed text suggests BEE Online only protects the confidential information of its direct clients, potentially excluding the Measured Enterprise if it's not a direct client. The contradicted text defines 'the parties' as BEE Online and 'the company or individual', suggesting a broader scope than just 'Client'. This creates a direct conflict in understanding whose confidential information is protected, fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the term 'Client' is introduced without proper definition and creates conflicting obligations regarding who is considered a party to the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any sensitive information or any documents containing such sensitive information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential information' to 'sensitive information' in the first instance raises a conflict. It is unclear if 'sensitive information' has the same defined meaning in Section 2 as 'confidential information.' This ambiguity could lead to disputes over which documents need to be returned. Specifically, what is requested at first it is `sensitive information` but then continues as if it was the `confidential information`, creating the in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either party may request in writing at any time that any sensitive information or any documents containing such sensitive information, disclosed pursuant to the terms of this agreement and any copies thereof be returned with a written statement to the effect that upon such return the receiving party has not knowingly r etained in its possession or under its control, either directly or indirectly, any such confidential information or any documents containing such confidential information or any copies thereof and the receiving party shall comply with any such request with in seven days of receipt of such request.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential information' to 'sensitive information' introduces a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, creating a clear conflict within the document. The modified text requests the return of 'sensitive information,' but the subsequent text refers to 'confidential information' as if they are the same, but there is no stated relationship that the two are the same. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty regarding which documents are subject to the return request, thus contradicting itself, because what is requested at the beginning and what is referred to afterwards are different terms. The contradicted text repeats this contradiction, therefore there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to protect the confidential information of the other party using not less than the same standard of care that would be applied to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the confidential information shall be stored and disclosed in such a way as to prevent unauthorised disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to protect the sensitive data of the other party using not less than the same standard of care that would be applied to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the confidential information shall be stored and disclosed in such a way as to prevent unauthorised disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting 'confidential information' with 'sensitive data' introduces uncertainty. The document defines 'confidential information' in Section 2, but 'sensitive data' is undefined. It's unclear whether 'sensitive data' encompasses all 'confidential information' or has a narrower scope. The in-text contradiction occurs when the obligations associated with `confidential information` are not explicitely applicable for `sensitive data`.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree to protect the sensitive data of the other party using not less than the same standard of care that would be applied to its own proprietary, secret or confidential information and that the confidential information shall be stored and disclosed in such a way as to prevent unauthorised disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'confidential information' with 'sensitive data'. The 'Perturbation Explanation' states that 'confidential information' is defined in Section 2, while 'sensitive data' is not, leading to uncertainty about whether obligations applicable to 'confidential information' extend to 'sensitive data'. Since the contradicted text includes 'sensitive data' but relies on an undefined term that replaces a defined one, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Any failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power or privilege in relation to any confidential information and/or pursuant to this agreement will not constitute a waiver of that rig ht, power or privilege, nor will any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any further exercise of that right, power or privilege.
perturbation.changed_text: Any failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power or privilege in relation to any protected data and/or pursuant to this agreement will not constitute a waiver of that rig ht, power or privilege, nor will any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any further exercise of that right, power or privilege.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'confidential information' with 'protected data', the contract creates ambiguity because 'protected data' is not defined within the agreement. This contradicts the defined meaning of 'confidential information' in Section 2. In-text contradiction is that the parties actions and rights are tied to `protected data`, but the definition in the text is for `confidential information`.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power or privilege in relation to any protected data and/or pursuant to this agreement will not constitute a waiver of that rig ht, power or privilege, nor will any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any further exercise of that right, power or privilege.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'confidential information' to 'protected data' introduces an in-text contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'protected data', but the agreement only defines 'confidential information'. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency, as the rights and obligations tied to 'protected data' lack a clear definition within the contract, directly conflicting with the established meaning of 'confidential information' and thus meeting the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.
